understanding.goal_understanding: The goal is to move selectable pushable pieces onto goal tiles marked '+' within a limited step budget per level, achieving a WIN condition reported by the engine to progress through levels and complete the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A single 'selected piece' is controlled at a time. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN shifts it one cell in the specified direction if unobstructed by walls or obstacles; otherwise, no movement. CLICK [x,y] attempts to relocate the selected piece directly to [x,y] if possible (unblocked/clear path), sometimes succeeding across distances, other times failing with no position change. No explicit player character or pushing mechanics observed; selection persists until changed, and actions deplete a finite step count per level.

orient_history: This is a selection-based puzzle where you select a 'selectable' object (which acts as the current player/piece) and move it cell-by-cell (3 pixels) in cardinal directions if not blocked by walls or other collidables. Goals are non-collidable but may block movement until a piece is properly placed on them. Success requires maneuvering selectables onto all goal tiles. CLICK action selects a different selectable by targeting its position.

Multiple collidable selectable pieces exist; one is always selected and controlled as the player. CLICK on an empty position teleports the selected piece there instantly (ignoring obstacles). CLICK on a selectable piece's position switches selection to it (no teleport). MOVE_* attempts to move the selected piece one cell (3 pixels) in that direction if unblocked; likely pushes adjacent unselected selectables or enters non-collidable goals. Win by positioning selectables on all goals.

Previous actions only swapped positions between [9,21] and [18,21] via overlap-and-move, wasting steps. No pushing occurred as not adjacent properly.
